posts.text:
THE DOD JUST SELECTED TWO BASES FOR NUCLEAR MICROREACTORS. THE RACE TO 2030 IS ON.

· Buckley Space Force Base and Malmstrom Air Force Base chosen under the Advanced Nuclear Power for Installations (ANPI) program

· Contractor-owned, contractor-operated model: industry will site, license, build, operate, and decommission on-base

· Target deployment: ~2030

· Next step: DOD will downselect from a pool of pre-qualified vendors based on mission and energy needs

THE ANPI VENDOR POOL INCLUDES:

· Antares Nuclear: sodium heat pipe microreactor
· Radiant Industries: Kaleidos 1 MWe HTGR
· X-energy: XENITH 3-10 MWe HTGR
· Oklo: Aurora fast reactor, also pursuing separate Eielson AFB pilot program
· BWXT Advanced Technologies: Project Pele prime contractor
· Westinghouse Government Services: eVinci microreactor
· General Atomics: compact HTGR concept
· Kairos Power: fluoride salt-cooled reactor

This is no longer conceptual.

The DOD has named sites, a deployment model, and a competitive vendor pool.

Execution risk remains, but the clock is real.

$OKLO $SMR $BWXT $CCJ
posts.author: quakes99
